title: ATAH Consent Receipt (full)
description: ATAH Core Protocol object: the full structured consent receipt issued by the asserting platform (spec/v0.8 §4.10). Replaces a boolean `consumer_consent` flag. ATAH supports two consent types — `query_authorization` and `disclosure_consent` — and explicitly excludes a third concept, `engagement_consent`, which covers any professional-client agreement, fee agreement, treatment consent, regulated advice consent, conflict waiver, scope-of-work acceptance, or instruction to act. Engagement consent is outside ATAH by design and MUST NOT be issued through this schema. ATAH consent receipts MUST NOT be represented as professional engagement consent. ATAH stores only the hash and continuity-binding metadata — see consent-receipt-stored.schema.json for the form ATAH retains. Required for any ATAH-conformant implementation.

consent_receipt_id.description: Opaque identifier issued at consent capture.

asserted.description: Whether the consent was given. A receipt with `asserted: false` records a refusal.

consent_type.description: Which of the two ATAH-supported consent types this receipt expresses. `query_authorization`: the user permits their AI to request candidate professionals. `disclosure_consent`: the user permits specific data to be shared with a specific professional for a specific introduction stage. The third concept `engagement_consent` is outside ATAH by design and is not a valid value here.

asserted_by.description: The party that captured and asserted the consent. Records authenticated_actor and (where the asserter is an AI platform) the client_application. Authority for the underlying consent ceremony is documented elsewhere; ATAH verifies receipt integrity, not the validity of the underlying consent.

consumer_ref.description: Opaque platform-side reference to the consumer. Not personally identifiable. ATAH does not learn the consumer identity from this field.

scope.description: The protocol action the consent covers. `query_submission` pairs with `consent_type: query_authorization`; the remaining values pair with `consent_type: disclosure_consent`. The professional-on-behalf-of-client case is served by Discovery alone with `request_intent: 'on_behalf_of_client'`; the referring professional handles the introduction off-platform under their own consent capture and does not use this schema for that step.

consent_text_version.description: Identifier of the canonical consent text the consumer was shown. Versions are published in the protocol documentation.
